_meta.title: Bit Cryptic World — Complete Clue Archive
_meta.description: Master database of every cryptic clue in the game as of 2026-04-13. This file is the authoritative archive — no clue should be deleted from the game without first being preserved here.

criticalIssues: daily_01: TAN — wordplay mechanism is broken. TAN is NOT hidden in 'tracked train takes in light'. Odd letters of TRAIN give T-A-N but 'tracked' is not a letter-selection indicator. NEEDS REPLACEMENT.
town_puzzle_02: LIBRARY — 'Quiet place where books live' is a straight definition, NOT a cryptic clue. No wordplay present. NEEDS REPLACEMENT.
library_puzzle_01: MAP — This is a traditional riddle, not a cryptic clue at all. NEEDS REPLACEMENT.
guide_homophone_02: SUR — 'SUR' is not a standard English word. The dictionary check passes but SUR is French/Spanish. NEEDS REPLACEMENT.
guide_reversal_01: THIN �� 'Take back the night by withholding first gold light' is a combined reversal+deletion, too complex for a teaching example. NEEDS SIMPLER REPLACEMENT.
minorIssues: forest_clue_02: LEAF — 'Flea dancing through a page' — 'through' is padding. It serves no cryptic function (neither indicator nor fodder). Surface is still acceptable.
observatory_puzzle_01: SKY — visual/shape puzzle, not a cryptic clue. Valid as a unique puzzle type but doesn't teach a cryptic technique.
daily_02_commented: CHIME — 'Church's first meter echos bright sound' — Wordplay is unclear. CH (church) + IME (meter?) — 'meter' doesn't clearly give IME. NEEDS REVIEW before uncommenting.
skyship_svg_01 vs skyship_interaction_01: SHIP — SVG says 'worship' but interaction says 'warship'. Both work but inconsistency should be resolved.

storyClues.definition: The whole reversed phrase is the definition — a greeting
storyClues.explanation: The entire postscript line is written backwards. '.S.P' = 'P.S.' reversed. 'EMOCLEW EMOH' = 'WELCOME HOME' reversed.
storyClues.speaker: Dock Keeper
storyClues.teachingDialogue: That line at the end reads .S.P which is just P.S. written backwards. The whole line is reversed. Give it a read the right way round.
storyClues.hint: Read the whole line backwards, starting from the right.
wrongReactions: Not quite. Take it letter by letter. EMOCLEW backwards is W-E-L-C-O-M-E. Now do the same for EMOH.
You've almost got it. EMOCLEW is WELCOME. EMOH is HOME. Put them together.
He smiles patiently and traces the letters in the air: 'W-E-L-C-O-M-E H-O-M-E. Two words. Say it like you mean it.'

storyClues.explanation: Hidden word. 'In' is the indicator. BARK is hidden spanning side-BAR K-nowledge. Definition: 'Yelp'.
storyClues.notes: Visual-only clue carved on ancient oak trunk in SVG. Not interactive — player reads it.

storyClues.definition: Page (a leaf = a page of a book)
storyClues.explanation: Hidden word. 'From' is the indicator. LEAF is hidden spanning who-LE AF-fair. Definition: 'Page'.
storyClues.notes: Visual-only clue carved on ancient oak trunk in SVG. The answer LEAF fits the forest theme.

storyClues.definition: Evergreen (a pine is an evergreen tree)
storyClues.explanation: Hidden word. 'From' is the indicator. PINE is hidden spanning worshi-P IN E-arnest. Definition: 'Evergreen'.
storyClues.notes: Visual-only clue carved on ancient oak trunk in SVG.

storyClues.clueText: Face remodelled into a place for coffee (4)
storyClues.explanation: 'Remodelled' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange FACE to get CAFE.
storyClues.speaker: The Letter Fox (via stone slab)
storyClues.teachingDialogue: The fox sits beside the stone and stares at you, tail flicking.
storyClues.hint: The answer is an anagram. Look for a word whose letters can be rearranged.
wrongReactions: The fox shakes its head and scratches at the word 'remodelled' on the stone.
The fox whines softly and nudges your hand. 'Remodelled' means the letters are shuffled. Which four-letter word can be rearranged?
The fox huffs and paws at 'face.' Rearrange those four letters into a place that serves drinks.

storyClues.clueText: Something instantly perky, starts a mouthful (3)
storyClues.explanation: 'Starts' is the initial-letters indicator. Take the first letter of Something, Instantly, Perky = S-I-P. Definition: 'a mouthful' (a sip of a drink).
storyClues.speaker: Barista
storyClues.teachingDialogue: That's an initial letters clue. 'Starts' tells you to take the first letter of each word before it. Three letters, and the definition is 'a mouthful.'
storyClues.hint: 'Starts' tells you to look at the beginning of each word.
wrongReactions: Not quite. Take the first letter of each word: Something, Instantly, Perky. What do those spell?
She traces the first letters on the counter with her finger: 'S... I... P...'
Three letters. S, I, P. What's a small mouthful of a drink?

storyClues.explanation: 'Mangled' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange WROTE to get TOWER.
storyClues.speaker: Town Crier (fountain water)
storyClues.teachingDialogue: Look for anagram indicators like 'mangled' and 'scrambled.'
storyClues.hint: Each clue describes a place on this island. Look for anagram indicators like 'mangled' and 'scrambled.'
wrongReactions: Not quite. For the first one, 'mangled' tells you the letters of 'wrote' are mixed up. What building can you make?
storyClues.notes: Part of a multi-puzzle set (3 clues solved in sequence)

previousClueTexts: Scales on railway, a place for books (7)
storyClues.definition: The entire clue is a cryptic definition — libraries are famously hushed, and they are collections
storyClues.explanation: Cryptic definition. The whole clue describes a LIBRARY obliquely: a 'hushed' place (libraries enforce quiet) that is a 'collection' (of books). The '?' signals something cryptic. Replaced the charade (LIBRA+RY) which was too obscure for this early stage.
storyClues.speaker: Town Crier (fountain water)
storyClues.hint: No tricks, just a description: where do books live?
wrongReactions: She taps the fountain. 'The second one is simpler. No tricks, just a description: where do books live?'
storyClues.notes: Part of the Town multi-puzzle. FIXED 2026-04-13: Replaced straight definition with cryptic definition 'Hushed collection? (7)'. Now validated and PASSED.

storyClues.clueText: Hop works, scrambled, a place to build (8)
storyClues.explanation: 'Scrambled' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange HOP WORKS to get WORKSHOP (8 letters).
storyClues.speaker: Town Crier (fountain water)
storyClues.hint: 'Scrambled' means rearrange 'hop works.' Eight letters, a place where you build things.
wrongReactions: For the third: 'scrambled' means rearrange 'hop works.' Eight letters, a place where you build things.

storyClues.explanation: 'Seized' is the hidden-word indicator. HERO is hidden inside 't-HERO-ad'. The definition is 'Warrior'.
storyClues.speaker: Tower Guardian
storyClues.teachingDialogue: Hidden word clue. The answer is concealed within the words themselves, spanning across them. Find it.
storyClues.hint: The answer is hiding across the words.
wrongReactions: Incorrect. Read the words again. The answer is spelled out across them, hidden in plain sight.
Look where one word ends and the next begins. The letters bridge the gap.
The answer sits across 'the road.' Four letters. Extract them.

storyClues.clueText: Ache about beginning of boating, on the shore (5)
storyClues.explanation: 'About' is the anagram indicator. Take the letters of ACHE + the first letter ('beginning of') BOATING = B. Rearrange B+A+C+H+E to get BEACH.
storyClues.speaker: Cryptic Croc
storyClues.teachingDialogue: In a cryptic clue, 'about' tells you the letters are jumbled. 'Beginning of boating' means take the first letter of 'boating.' Mix that with the letters of 'ache,' and you get a word that means 'on the shore.'
storyClues.hint: Jumble the letters of 'ache' with one more letter from the clue.
wrongReactions: Not quite, but you're thinking. 'Beginning of boating' gives you the letter B. Mix that with A, C, H, E.
You've got the letters B, E, A, C, H. What word meaning 'shore' can you make from those?
He writes the five letters on the workbench with chalk: B, E, A, C, H. 'Rearrange these. Where do you find sand and waves?'

storyClues.explanation: 'Broken' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange HORSE to get SHORE. Definition: 'on the coastline'.
storyClues.teachingDialogue: This is Scramble Shores, after all. 'Broken' tells you to rearrange the letters.
storyClues.hint: 'Broken' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange the letters of HORSE. The definition is 'coastline.'
wrongReactions: The crab clicks its claws impatiently. Rearrange the letters of 'horse.'
Five letters: H, O, R, S, E. What word meaning 'coastline' can you spell?
S-H-O-R-E. The horse, broken apart, becomes the shore beneath your feet.
storyClues.notes: NEW: Added as the first formal cryptic clue on Scramble Shores. The original TIFL OT NEPO choice-based puzzle remains later in the story.

storyClues.clueText: Trap set up for a piece of the whole (4)
storyClues.explanation: 'Set up' is the reversal indicator. Reverse TRAP to get PART. Definition: 'a piece of the whole'.
storyClues.teachingDialogue: A reversal clue. 'Set up' tells you to reverse a word. The definition is 'a piece of the whole.'
storyClues.hint: One of these words reads differently backwards.
wrongReactions: The lock buzzes. 'Look for the reversal indicator. Which word tells you to read something backwards?'
A crab pinches your toe. 'Set up' means reversed. Which four-letter word in the clue can be flipped?
Reverse the word 'trap.' What do you get?

storyClues.explanation: 'Hidden in' is the indicator. CHEST is hidden inside 'eaCHESTimate'. Definition: 'Treasure'.
storyClues.speaker: The Dragon
storyClues.teachingDialogue: A hidden word clue. Simple enough. But the next requires a different kind of thinking.
storyClues.hint: The answer is hiding inside the words of the clue itself. Read across the word boundaries.
wrongReactions: Look at the words more carefully. The answer is hidden in plain sight.
Read the letters across 'each estimate' slowly. What word do you see?
E-A-C-H-E-S-T-I-M-A-T-E. The answer spans two words.

storyClues.clueText: This beast makes proceedings drag on (6)
storyClues.definition: This beast / makes proceedings drag on — both define DRAGON
storyClues.explanation: 'This beast' = DRAGON (the creature). 'Makes proceedings drag on' = DRAGON (to drag on means to go slowly, and 'drag on' literally spells DRAGON).
storyClues.speaker: The Dragon
storyClues.teachingDialogue: A double definition. You have sharp eyes. But the final clue is my finest. It requires you to rearrange.
storyClues.hint: This is a double definition. Both halves of the clue define the same word. What beast am I?
wrongReactions: Two definitions in one clue. What creature guards this lair?
Think about what 'drag on' means as two separate words. Now think of a beast.
DRAG ON means to bore. What mythical creature sounds exactly like that?

storyClues.clueText: Fun race, oddly, leads to the source of heat (7)
storyClues.explanation: 'Oddly' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange FUN RACE (7 letters) to get FURNACE. Definition: 'the source of heat'.
storyClues.speaker: The Dragon
storyClues.hint: 'Oddly' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange the letters of two words. 'Source of heat' is the definition.
wrongReactions: Oddly tells you to rearrange some letters. Which letters?
Take the letters F-U-N-R-A-C-E. Jumble them up. What source of heat can you make?
Rearrange FUN RACE into a 7-letter word. Think of something hot.

storyClues.definition: Blemish / a target — both define MARK
storyClues.explanation: Double definition. MARK = a blemish/stain AND MARK = a target to aim at. Also the character's name.
storyClues.speaker: Mark
storyClues.teachingDialogue: Yes, that's my name and my nature. I leave my mark on every puzzle.
storyClues.hint: This is a double definition. Both halves of the clue point to the same four-letter word. A blemish on a surface, and something you aim at.
wrongReactions: Two definitions, one word. Think about what a blemish is. Think about what a target is.
A stain or scratch on a surface. Something you aim for. Four letters.
It's also a name. My name, in fact.

storyClues.explanation: 'Confused' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange TRACER to get CRATER. Definition: 'Lunar feature'.
storyClues.speaker: Mark
storyClues.teachingDialogue: Impressive. You solved my crater clue.
storyClues.hint: 'Confused' is the anagram indicator. Rearrange the letters of TRACER. The definition is 'lunar feature'.
wrongReactions: The word confused tells you to jumble up some letters. Which letters?
Take T-R-A-C-E-R and rearrange them. What lunar feature can you spell?
An anagram of TRACER gives you a six-letter word for a hole on the moon's surface.

storyClues.definition: The stars form letter shapes: S (curved line), K (vertical with horizontals), Y (line forking at top)
storyClues.explanation: A visual puzzle. Three constellations form the shapes of the letters S, K, Y. Not a traditional cryptic clue.
storyClues.speaker: Star Watcher
storyClues.teachingDialogue: Up here, the universe writes in light. Softly. Patiently. Shall we read together?
storyClues.hint: Read the shapes as letters.
wrongReactions: Look again. The first curve. You've seen that shape on the page before. It bends and flows.
'S.' She traces it in the air. 'Then a crossed line. K. Then a forking branch. What letter splits at the top?'
She places her hand gently on yours. 'S. K. Y. Three letters. Where are you looking right now?'

storyClues.clueText: Head of household? Ring me, I live here! (4)
storyClues.explanation: 'Head of household' = H (first letter). 'Ring' = O (circular shape, standard crossword abbreviation). 'Me' = ME. H+O+ME = HOME. Definition: 'I live here!'
storyClues.hint: 'Head of' means take the first letter. 'Ring' is a common crossword word for a round shape.
wrongReactions: 'Head of household' gives you the letter H. What shape is a ring?
H, then O (a ring), then ME. Put them together.
H-O-M-E. The head of Household, a ring (O), and ME.

storyClues.explanation: 'Conceals' is the hidden-word indicator. VOYAGE is hidden inside 'sa-VOYAGE-nt'. Definition: 'a journey'.
storyClues.speaker: Simon
storyClues.teachingDialogue: It's a hidden word clue. The answer is tucked inside the words themselves, hiding across the gap between two words. See if you can spot it.
storyClues.hint: The answer hides inside the surrounding words, spanning across the gap. Look at where 'savoy' ends and 'agent' begins.
wrongReactions: Not quite. Read the letters across the two words slowly: S-A-V-O-Y-A-G-E-N-T. See a journey hiding in there?
Start from the 'V' in 'savoy' and read six letters forward: V-O-Y-A-G-E.
saVOYAGEnt. Six letters, right there in the middle. A journey.

storyClues.definition: Stagger / a fishing spool — both define REEL
storyClues.explanation: Double definition. REEL = to stagger/sway AND REEL = a fishing spool that holds line.
storyClues.hint: Think of a word that means both 'to stagger' and 'a spool used for fishing line.'
wrongReactions: Two definitions, one answer. What do you call it when someone staggers or sways?
Think about what holds the fishing line. A spool that you wind. It also means to stumble.
R-E-E-L. You reel in a fish, and you reel from a shock. Four letters.

storyClues.previousClueText: I have cities but no houses. I have mountains but no trees. I have water but no fish. I have roads but no cars. What am I?
storyClues.explanation: Double definition. MAP = a chart (of territory) AND MAP = to plan (map out a route). Replaced the original traditional riddle with a proper cryptic clue. Library now teaches double definitions.
storyClues.speaker: Head Librarian
storyClues.hint: Something that represents the world without being the world itself.
wrongReactions: Not quite. Think about what shows you these things without actually containing them.
She glances at a rolled-up parchment on her desk. 'You use one of these every time you navigate somewhere new.'
Three letters. Flat, made of paper, and very useful when you're lost.
storyClues.notes: FIXED 2026-04-13: Replaced traditional riddle with double definition 'Chart or plan (3)'. Now validated and PASSED.

svgOnlyClues.clueText: Boat hidden in worship, I presume (4)
svgOnlyClues.explanation: 'Hidden in' is the indicator. SHIP is hidden inside 'wor-SHIP'. Definition: 'Boat'.
svgOnlyClues.notes: Displayed on the newspaper/gazette SVG in the cabin. Text only — no interactive puzzle. The index.html interaction version at line 3507 uses 'warship' instead of 'worship'.

svgOnlyClues.explanation: 'Found in' is the hidden-word indicator. IRE is hidden inside 'des-IRE'. Definition: 'Anger'.
svgOnlyClues.notes: Displayed on the newspaper/gazette SVG. Text only — no interactive puzzle.

interactionClues.notes: Alert-based interaction (Unscramble Shells button). Not a formal puzzle — just an alert dialog.

interactionClues.explanation: Unscramble each word: DLROW=WORLD, CITPRYC=CRYPTIC, TIB=BIT → BIT CRYPTIC WORLD.
interactionClues.notes: Alert-based interaction (Message in a Bottle button). Not a formal puzzle.
Duplicate of lair_puzzle_01 — shown as an alert when clicking 'Face the Dragon's Clue' interaction button.

interactionClues.clueText: This beast makes proceedings drag on (6)
interactionClues.notes: Duplicate of lair_puzzle_02 — shown as alert when clicking 'Search the Hoard' interaction button.

interactionClues.clueText: Fun race, oddly, leads to the source of heat (7)
interactionClues.notes: Duplicate of lair_puzzle_03 — shown as alert when clicking 'Read the Wall Carvings' interaction button.
Duplicate of moon_puzzle_01 — shown as alert when clicking 'Meet Mark' interaction button.
Duplicate of moon_puzzle_02 — shown as alert when clicking 'Mark's Esoteric Clue' interaction button.

interactionClues.clueText: Boat hidden in worship, I presume (4)
interactionClues.notes: Alert-based interaction. FIXED 2026-04-15: Standardized to 'worship' to match SVG version.
Duplicate of skyship_svg_02 — shown as alert when clicking 'Browse Simon's Collection' interaction button.

dailyGameClues.explanation: ORIGINAL CLAIM: TAN hidden in 'tracked train takes'. VALIDATION FAILED: TAN does not appear as a consecutive string anywhere in the clue text. The odd-positioned letters of TRAIN (T,A,N) spell TAN, but 'tracked' is not a valid letter-selection indicator. This clue has broken wordplay and MUST be replaced.
dailyGameClues.notes: CRITICAL: Broken wordplay. The only active clue in the daily game. Must be replaced with a verified clue.

dailyGameClues.clueText: Church's first meter echos bright sound
dailyGameClues.explanation: CH (Church's first two letters) + IME (meter/rhythm) = CHIME. Definition: 'bright sound'.
dailyGameClues.notes: COMMENTED OUT in source code. Not currently active.

wordPlayGuideExamples.clueText: A foreign car buried in the tennis sand (6)
wordPlayGuideExamples.notes: Hidden in 'ten-NISSAN-d'. Correct but the word-boundary split is confusing.

wordPlayGuideExamples.clueText: Refined portion of garden within hedge shearing (5)
Take back the night by witholding first gold light (4)
wordPlayGuideExamples.notes: Complex: NIGHT reversed = THGIN, delete G (first of Gold) = THIN. Too complex for a teaching example.

wordPlayGuideExamples.clueText: Even stripes can be formal wear (3)
Ones wearing red, orange, yellow, and lilac can be crowned (5)
The number sounded not against it (4)
The Mountain heard a noble title (3)
wordPlayGuideExamples.notes: Questionable: 'SUR' is not standard English. Intended as 'sounds like SIR' but SUR is French/Spanish for 'south'. Consider replacing.
